LAW AND MOTION 302, HEARING RE: DEFENDANT LYFT, INC.'S RENEWED MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND TO STAY. THIS MATTER IS CONTESTED. ARGUMENT IS HELD. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT LYFT, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PROCEEDINGS IS GRANTED. Notice Of Renewed Motion And Renewed Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay
Off Calendar Oct-25-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
Lawrence M. Willhalm, Tel. 510.892.5552, Plaintiff and Self-Represented Litigant;
Cara F. Barrick, Esq. of Ogletree Deakins Nash Smoak & Stewart P.C., Tel. 415.442.4810, appearing for Defendant Lyft, Inc.;
Jenny J. Liao, Esq. of of Ogletree Deakins Nash Smoak & Stewart P.C., Tel. 415.442.4810, appearing for Defendant Lyft, Inc.

Hearing re: DEFENDANT LYFT, INC.'S Renewed Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay.

This matter is contested. Argument is held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Lyft, Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration and stay proceedings is granted.

(See Word minutes for the complete ruling.)

Court to review the proposed order submitted by the prevailing party.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Court Reporter: Rudd, CSR #. Bailiff: Petrucci. (301/RCE)

Defendant Lyft, Inc.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
